basic_details.job_title: CGPSC Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 15 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission (CGPSC) has released Advertisement No. 03/2026/Exam dated 17 June 2026 to fill 15 posts of Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari (Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer) in the Home Department, Government of Chhattisgarh. The online application window opens on 22 June 2026 (12:00 PM) and closes on 21 July 2026 (11:59 PM), with selection through a written exam and interview, and pay at Level-9 (Rs. 38,100–1,20,400) of the pay matrix.

application_fee.details: Candidates asking how much the CGPSC ADPPO 2026 application fee costs can note that it varies by category and domicile status, with an additional portal charge applicable to everyone. Local residents of Chhattisgarh belonging to SC, ST, or OBC (non-creamy layer), as well as local disabled candidates, pay Rs. 300, while all other candidates, including those from outside Chhattisgarh, pay Rs. 400; in both cases, a portal fee plus GST is charged additionally to all applicants. | Candidates should also note that local residents of Chhattisgarh who actually appear in the written exam or interview will get their examination fee refunded to the bank account entered during the application process, though the portal fee, GST, and any correction charges are never refunded.

qualification.details: Candidates wondering about the qualification and age required for this post can note that CGPSC requires a Bachelor's degree in Law from a recognized university, along with an age between 21 and 30 years as on 1 January 2026, with category-specific relaxations available. Meeting these minimum criteria does not automatically guarantee a call for the exam or interview, since the Commission can further screen candidates based on these or higher qualifications. | Candidates must hold a Bachelor's Degree in Law (LLB) from a university recognized by the UGC. The post is classified as Gazetted, Second Class, and selected candidates are appointed on a 3-year probation period. Candidates must hold the required educational qualification certificate by the last date of online application; degrees awarded after the closing date will not be considered valid. | The standard age limit is minimum 21 years and maximum 30 years as on 1 January 2026, based on the date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or equivalent certificate. However, considering the interest of educated unemployed youth, the State Government grants local residents of Chhattisgarh a 5-year relaxation on the special upper limit of 35 years, effectively allowing local candidates to apply up to 40 years of age (this relaxation period currently runs from 1 January 2024 to 31 December 2028). | On top of this, additional relaxations in the upper age limit are available as follows: | SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) of Chhattisgarh: 5 years relaxation on the higher age limit. Government/corporation employees of Chhattisgarh (regular, temporary, or work-charge): upper age limit capped at 38 years. Ex-servicemen of Chhattisgarh: relaxation equal to defence service period, but the resulting age must not exceed the higher limit by more than 3 years. Retrenched government servants: relaxation for past temporary service (up to 7 years), but resulting age must not exceed the higher limit by more than 3 years. Widowed, divorced, or separated women of Chhattisgarh: 5 years relaxation. Contractual employees of Chhattisgarh: relaxation equal to years of contractual service, capped at age 38. Persons with disabilities: 5 years relaxation. Shiksha Karmi / Panchayat Karmi: relaxation equal to years of service, capped at age 45. | Candidates should note that all relaxations combined cannot push the maximum age limit beyond 45 years, and reservation/age-relaxation benefits are available only to bonafide local/domicile residents of Chhattisgarh. | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

salary_details.details: Candidates asking about the salary for this post can note that the Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari post carries Pay Matrix Level-9, with a pay scale of Rs. 38,100 to Rs. 1,20,400, in addition to Dearness Allowance and other allowances as notified by the State Government from time to time. This pay scale applies uniformly to both the regular and carry-forward/backlog vacancies advertised under this post. | In-Hand Salary Calculator

selection_process: Stage Component Marks / Details
1 Written Exam (Objective Type) 150 questions, 3 hours, 300 marks total
1A Part 1 – General Knowledge 50 questions, 100 marks
1B Part 2 – Law (Major Acts + Minor Acts) 100 questions, 200 marks
2 Interview 30 marks
Final Merit List Combined exam + interview marks (330 total), category-wise
Candidates curious about how CGPSC shortlists the Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer can note that selection happens in two stages: a written exam followed by an interview
with the written exam carrying 300 marks and the interview carrying 30 marks
for a combined total of 330 marks. The written exam consists of a single objective-type paper of 150 questions to be completed in 3 hours
with negative marking for wrong answers.
The exam paper is bilingual (Hindi and English)
and the Commission may conduct it as a Computer Based Test (CBT) depending on the number of applicants. Negative marking is applied using the formula MO = M × R − (1/3) × M × W
where MO is marks obtained
M is marks per correct answer
R is the number of correct answers
and W is the number of wrong answers. To qualify

candidates must score a minimum of 33% in each paper for Unreserved category candidates and 23% for reserved category candidates; there is no minimum qualifying mark for the interview. Candidates called for interview will be approximately three times the number of vacancies
drawn only from those declared qualified in the written exam
and the written exam will be held at centres in Ambikapur
Bilaspur
Raipur
Durg
and Jagdalpur (subject to change based on the number of applicants).
Also Read: ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 – 17 Junior Research Fellow Posts
how_to_apply: Candidates wondering how to submit their application can follow the numbered steps below, all of which take place online through the official CGPSC portal. Importantly, no certificates need to be uploaded at the time of online application, since document verification happens only at a later stage.
Visit the official CGPSC website at www.psc.cg.gov.in.

First-time applicants must complete "Candidate's Registration" by entering name, father's name, mother's name, gender, date of birth, mobile number, email ID, and ID proof number, then generate and verify the OTP sent to the registered mobile/email.

Capture a live photo using the mobile camera link sent after OTP verification - ensure the face is clear, both ears are visible, eyes are open, and the background is a single light colour, with the face covering about 80% of the frame.
Sign on a plain white sheet with a black ball-point pen, scan only the signature (not the blank background), and upload the signature as a JPG file (maximum 100 KB).
Click Save, then go to the dashboard and click "Apply" against the Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari post.
Carefully fill in all required details in the online application form.
Pay the application fee plus portal fee and GST through the available online payment modes (credit card, debit card, net banking, UPI, etc.).
Confirm that the Application Status shows "Submitted" and the Payment Status shows "Paid" on the receipt page.
Download and save the application receipt and payment receipt, since these will be required at later stages of the selection process.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for CGPSC Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application and pay the fee is 21 July 2026 at 11:59 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in this CGPSC recruitment?
faq.answer: There are 15 total vacancies - 10 regular and 5 carry-forward/backlog posts - distributed across UR, SC, ST, and OBC categories.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for CGPSC Assistant District Public Prosecution Officer?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Bachelor's Degree in Law from a recognized university and must be between 21 and 30 years of age as on 1 January 2026, with relaxations available for specific categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for CGPSC ADPPO 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is Rs. 300 for local SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) and local disabled candidates of Chhattisgarh, and Rs. 400 for all other candidates, plus a portal fee and GST for everyone.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for this post?
faq.answer: Selection is through a written exam (300 marks) covering General Knowledge and Law, followed by an interview (30 marks), with the final merit list based on the combined 330 marks.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary of Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari?
faq.answer: The post carries Pay Matrix Level-9 with a pay scale of Rs. 38,100 to Rs. 1,20,400, plus applicable allowances, and is a gazetted second-class post with a 3-year probation period.

faq.question: Q7. When is the CGPSC ADPPO 2026 exam likely to be held?
faq.answer: The probable exam date is 20 September 2026, as mentioned in the official notification.

faq.question: Q8. Can I correct mistakes in my CGPSC application after submission?
faq.answer: Yes, a free correction window runs from 22 to 24 July 2026, and a paid correction window (Rs. 500 fee) runs from 25 to 26 July 2026, but corrections are limited only to gender, date of birth, category, domicile, disability, and ex-servicemen status.

faq.question: Q9. Do I need to upload documents with the online application?
faq.answer: No, no certificates need to be uploaded at the application stage; documents are verified only when a candidate is shortlisted for the interview.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website for CGPSC Sahayak Jila Lok Abhiyojan Adhikari 2026?
faq.answer: The official website is www.psc.cg.gov.in, where candidates can apply online and check all updates related to this recruitment.
